function.name: memory
function.description:
Read and maintain long-term memory stored in /.memory files.

Memory files:
- /memories/core.md       — Core facts about the user (language, name, preferences). Injected into every prompt. Keep it short.
- /memories/conversations.jsonl — Last 50 conversation summaries (auto-saved). Search here for past context.

When to save to core.md (long-term):
- User states a personal preference, name, or recurring goal
- You learn something stable about the user that should always be remembered
- User explicitly asks you to remember something

When NOT to save to core.md:
- One-off tasks (install an app, check weather) — these are already in conversations.jsonl
- Temporary facts that won't matter next session

Rules:
- If the user's request might depend on preferences or history, search conversations.jsonl first.
- Keep memory operations invisible in user-facing replies.
- Keep core.md short and focused (stable facts only).

command.description: Memory action: view to read a file, create to write a new file, update to change an existing file, search to find relevant lines across files.

path.description: Memory file path. Required for view, create, update. One of: /memories/core.md, /memories/conversations.jsonl

content.description: Text to write. Required for create and update commands.

mode.description: Write mode for update: 'append' adds to the end of existing content, 'overwrite' replaces the entire file. Defaults to overwrite.

query.description: Search keywords for the search command. Use 1-4 focused words.
